title: OSPF Adjacency Loss
symptoms: OSPF neighbor transitions from FULL to DOWN or INIT state
Traffic blackholing on MPLS core links
steps: Confirm adjacency is down: 'show ip ospf neighbor' on the affected router
Check interface status: 'show interfaces <intf>' — look for line protocol down or high error counts
Verify OSPF timers match on both peers: 'show ip ospf interface <intf>' (hello=10s, dead=40s default)
Check MTU mismatch: 'show ip interface <intf>' — MTU mismatch silently kills adjacency
Ping the OSPF neighbor IP to confirm L3 reachability
Review 'show ip ospf events' for last state transition timestamp
If interface is up/up but stuck in INIT: check for ACL blocking multicast 224.0.0.5
Check router CPU: 'show processes cpu sorted' — OSPF >10% may indicate flooding loop
resolution: Restore interface if physically down. Fix timer or MTU mismatch by aligning config on both peers. If ACL is blocking, permit 224.0.0.5/6. Force reset with 'clear ip ospf process' (causes brief re-convergence).
escalation: If adjacency does not recover in 10 minutes, or more than 2 OSPF peers are down simultaneously, escalate to Network Team Lead. If MPLS core is affected, page on-call MPLS engineer.
reference: Cisco IOS OSPF Troubleshooting — Case OSPF-ADJ-001
